title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers human reports, machine output, terminal width, and accessibility into a changed accessible human and machine report system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of accessible human and machine report system with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Report Information Architecture
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 7934 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Report Information Architecture.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Report Information Architecture, keep Report Information Architecture connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-1tljxy
# Competency: bookbot-structured-alternative.
# Case 4197: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_1tljxy(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1tljxy = "1tljxy-yxjlt1-4197"
    assert evidence_variant_1tljxy
    evidence_axes_1tljxy = (
        "corpus1tljxy",
        "policyyxjlt1",
        "revision4197",
        "command1tljxy4197",
        "reportyxjlt14197",
        "test41971tljxy",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1tljxy)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"
